local-18.body: Still blocked on live external posting policy. Recommended minimal unblock: default coordination_request to draft-only everywhere; allow live GitHub/GitLab/Jira comments only when the project config explicitly names the provider surface and target issue/PR/MR, with no auto-approval, no merge action, and no repeated pings.

External posting policy investigation update: prefer draft-only by default. If using GitHub as the shared tracker, live comments/status updates can be allowed only from a dedicated bot/GitHub App to named issues/PRs/projects; no auto-approval, merge, repeated pings, or writes outside the configured coordination repo/project.

User direction update: automation should not act as the user personal GitHub account. Prefer a visible bot identity, ideally via a GitHub App or least-privilege dedicated machine user in a custom private organization. Live writes should stay scoped to the coordination org/repo/project.

Brainstormed live external coordination policy notes and recorded them in `docs/shared-multi-host-coordination-prd.md`.

Key direction captured:
- provider actions should be capability-scoped: draft, post, assign, label, ping, review, merge, close, and publish are not the same permission.
- likely coordination events include error triage, solution agreement, option choice, ownership transfer, permission/runtime approval, PR/MR review, review response, CI ambiguity, integration conflicts, scope changes, API/config/dependency changes, release/rollback, stale waits, upstream dependency decisions, and human escalation.
- assignment is useful as an ownership/expected-responder signal, but should not be treated as approval by default.
- approval should come from provider-native review approval, configured label/comment/status transition, and recorded provider object/actor/timestamp evidence.
- silence should become waiting/timed_out/blocked, not implicit approval.

Remaining HITL decision: decide the first live-provider policy matrix before enabling external posting beyond draft/mocked flows.

Additional policy decision from user review:

- The coordinator can make most ordinary event-handling decisions by default, and dogfood experience will refine the policy over time.
- Every external coordination event should update the DevNexus project state machine: work-item status/labels, target-cycle facts, notes, continuation decisions, or publication gates.
- Assignment is not approval. Assignment means ownership or expected responder.
- Approval should be represented by explicit provider state transitions. For GitHub, prefer labels or GitHub Projects status/category moves for issue-level approval; for pull requests, require provider-native review approval plus required checks and DevNexus publication policy.

Recorded this in `docs/shared-multi-host-coordination-prd.md` and target-state. Remaining live-posting blockers are concrete provider mapping, credentials/test target, and rate-limit/ping defaults before non-draft external mutations.

local-62.body:
Completed and published.

Root cause:
- On Windows, bare `codex` resolved to the WindowsApps alias `C:\Program Files\WindowsApps\OpenAI.Codex_...\app\resources\codex.exe`.
- That alias fails from this host shell with `Acc?s refus?`.
- The user-local Codex Desktop binary `C:\Users\gabriel.darbord\AppData\Local\OpenAI\Codex\bin\codex.exe` runs successfully.

Fix:
- DevNexus now keeps shared profiles portable with `command: codex`.
- When resolving a Codex executor profile on Windows, DevNexus uses the user-local Codex Desktop CLI if present, or `DEV_NEXUS_CODEX_COMMAND` when explicitly set.
- Resolved command paths are shell-quoted, so paths with spaces are safe.
- The CLI test no longer assumes a host-invariant executable string.

Published source/package:
- DevNexus commit `8358ad8` pushed to `origin/main`.
- Published `@evref-bl/dev-nexus@0.1.0-alpha.13` on npm with the `dogfood` tag.
- Refreshed this dogfood root's local runtime install so `.dev-nexus/runtime/npm-tools/node_modules/dev-nexus` is alpha.13.

Verification:
- Focused: `npm test -- src/nexusAutomationAgentProfile.test.ts` passed.
- Full: `npm run check` passed in the implementation worktree and again in the publish checkout: 48 test files, 327 tests.
- Runtime smoke: installed package resolves the dogfood coordinator profile to `C:\Users\gabriel.darbord\AppData\Local\OpenAI\Codex\bin\codex.exe`, and the command runner executes that binary with `--version` successfully.

local-77.body:
Completed issue slicing for docs/remote-host-execution-prd.md.

Created implementation and rollout slices:
- dev-nexus:local-79 ready: host registry and host-local overlay model.
- dev-nexus:local-80 todo/dependent: runner profile schema and safety gates.
- dev-nexus:local-81 todo/dependent: remote execution request/result records.
- dev-nexus:local-82 todo/dependent: read-only host capability checks.
- dev-nexus:local-83 todo/dependent: SSH transport planning with mocked tests.
- dev-nexus:local-84 todo/dependent: verification-only remote command execution with identity guardrails.
- dev-nexus:local-85 blocked/HITL: choose first Mac/Windows remote runner dogfood policy.
- dev-nexus:local-86 blocked/HITL: run first read-only Mac/Windows remote host status smoke.
- dev-nexus-pharo:local-14 todo/dependent: plugin host capability probes and runner profile templates.
- plexus:local-23 todo/dependent: align isolated live-smoke runner boundary with generic remote runner profiles.

Sequencing decision: dev-nexus:local-79 is the first autonomous implementation slice. Live SSH/Tailscale, Pharo runtime, Docker, package install, GUI, publication, and destructive cleanup behavior remain dependent or blocked until policy and runner profiles are explicit.

Verification: work-item creation completed through the DevNexus CLI; no live network, SSH, Tailscale, Pharo image, Docker, package install, or destructive cleanup was run.

local-78.body: Product Requirements Document drafted: docs/coordination-roles-authority-prd.md. It defines the actor/role/action authority model for maintainer, contributor, reviewer, observer, runtime operator, and release operator flows; expected command gating; provider approval signal mapping; target-state effects; and implementation slicing for dev-nexus:local-78.

Sliced docs/coordination-roles-authority-prd.md into component-owned DevNexus work items:

- dev-nexus:local-87 Add actor role and authority configuration model (ready)
- dev-nexus:local-88 Resolve current actor from host-local auth profiles (dependent)
- dev-nexus:local-89 Add effective authority resolver (dependent)
- dev-nexus:local-90 Surface effective authority in status reports and agent context (dependent)
- dev-nexus:local-91 Gate publication actions with authority checks (dependent)
- dev-nexus:local-92 Gate coordination and provider mutation actions with authority checks (dependent)
- dev-nexus:local-93 Map provider approval and branch-policy signals into authority state (dependent)
- dev-nexus:local-94 Document authority roles for private bots and open-source contributors (dependent)
- dev-nexus:local-95 Decide advanced authority policies for self-approval and temporary elevation (blocked/HITL)

Implementation should start with local-87. Keep live provider behavior mocked or policy-gated, do not infer approval from assignment or comments, and keep local-78 as the umbrella planning item.

Parallel-agent Git workflow slicing cross-check completed.

The new parallel-agent items are dev-nexus:local-96 through dev-nexus:local-103. They intentionally do not duplicate the authority model from this item family:
- Actor identity, roles, action grants, effective authority, publication gating, provider mutation gating, and approval signal mapping remain owned by dev-nexus:local-87 through dev-nexus:local-95.
- Parallel-agent items own work ownership and safety mechanics: shared-checkout mutation boundaries, advisory worktree leases, start/adopt flow, coordination visibility for active branches, and cleanup dry-run/execution safety.
- Publication/direct integration behavior should continue through dev-nexus:local-91, and provider mutation/review behavior through dev-nexus:local-92.

The parallel-agent PRD now has an explicit Relationship To Authority section so future implementation work consumes effective authority rather than introducing a second role model.

Reclassifying this umbrella planning item as complete. Evidence: docs/coordination-roles-authority-prd.md is drafted and attached; it has been sliced into local-87 through local-95; local-87 has landed the first authority configuration model. Keeping this umbrella item ready would keep eligible-work selecting planning work that is already represented by child items.

Worker branch: codex/dev-nexus-local-87-authority-model

Status at handoff: ready

Decisions reported by worker: added a neutral declarative authority model only; no command gating, publication behavior changes, live provider permission checks, or credential storage. Recommended roles are built in, project-defined roles can add or replace definitions by id during normalization, and unknown actors resolve to the configured fallback role defaulting to observer.

This is now superseded by the merged handoff for DevNexus main at ba9976e.

local-98.body: Scope strengthened after review: this should be a fail-closed tool feature, not policy text. DevNexus-controlled write operations should classify the target checkout and refuse shared project/component checkout mutation before writing. First implementation should not wait for the full authority model; use explicit command/action allowlists and narrow bootstrap/integration overrides, then let authority refine those overrides later.

Implemented and published fail-closed shared-checkout mutation guard as DevNexus commit e3ab2de. Added checkout classification, mutation decisions, CLI guard calls for mutating command paths, MCP guard payloads for guarded calls, bootstrap/integration/allow overrides, and focused CLI/MCP coverage. Verification: npm run check passed with 57 test files and 451 tests.

local-117.body: PRD created at `docs/project-hosting-provisioning-prd.md`. Key product decision: keep the declared API minimal (repository, remotes, required access, provisioning gates) while provider adapters own repository creation, collaborator repair, pending invitation detection, and invitee-profile invitation acceptance. Ready for `$to-issues` slicing.

Sliced the hosting provisioning PRD into `dev-nexus:local-118` through `dev-nexus:local-127`.

Order:
- `local-118` access declarations and provisioning gates
- `local-119` read-only hosting status
- `local-120` deterministic hosting plan actions
- `local-121` CLI/MCP status and plan surfaces
- `local-122` local remote repair apply
- `local-123` GitHub repository creation apply
- `local-124` GitHub collaborator repair and pending invite detection
- `local-125` invitee-profile invitation acceptance
- `local-126` setup/status documentation integration
- `local-127` opt-in fake-project GitHub hosting integration test

Important correction from review: live hosting validation must use a disposable fake project/repository fixture, not `dev-nexus-plexus` or any other active project.
Closing as done: docs/project-hosting-provisioning-prd.md has been sliced into implementation items local-118 through local-127; local-118 is the next ready slice.

local-118.body: Completed and published to DevNexus main as 180955f. Added hosting access principal declarations, explicit provisioning gates, validation for duplicate/invalid principals, and shared-config secret field rejection. Verification: npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts src/nexusProjectConfig.test.ts src/nexusSetupAssistant.test.ts passed; npm run check passed with 57 files and 451 tests.

local-119.body: Promoted to ready after dev-nexus:local-118 landed as 180955f. The hosting config model now has access declarations and provisioning gates, so this read-only status-model slice can proceed.

Completed and published to DevNexus main as 7f53a03. Added a pure read-only hosting status model covering repository facts, expected/local remote match state, auth profile actor observations, required access, pending invitations, and structured warnings/blockers. Verification: npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts passed; npm run check passed with 57 files and 454 tests.

local-120.body: Promoted to ready after dev-nexus:local-118 and dev-nexus:local-119 landed as 180955f and 7f53a03. The status input needed for deterministic dry-run plan actions is now available.

Completed and published to DevNexus main as 4fa962f. Added deterministic hosting plan actions over the read-only status model, including repository creation, local remote repair, collaborator add/update/invite, invitation acceptance or manual wait, default-branch repair, visibility repair safety, and unsupported-provider blockers. Verification: npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts passed; npm run build passed; npm run check passed with 57 files and 457 tests.

local-121.body: Promoted to ready after dev-nexus:local-119 and dev-nexus:local-120 landed as 7f53a03 and 4fa962f. The read-only status model and deterministic plan model are now available for CLI/MCP exposure.

Completed and published to DevNexus main as 37717f7. Added read-only `dev-nexus project hosting status` and `dev-nexus project hosting plan` CLI commands plus `project_hosting_status` and `project_hosting_plan` MCP tools. JSON output preserves structured status and plan details; text output gives compact summaries; commands do not mutate remotes or provider state. Verification: npm test -- src/cli.test.ts src/nexusMcpServer.test.ts passed with 65 tests; npm run build passed; npm run check passed with 57 files and 459 tests.

local-122.body: Promoted to ready after dev-nexus:local-120 and dev-nexus:local-121 landed as 4fa962f and 37717f7. The deterministic dry-run plan and CLI/MCP status/plan surfaces are now available; local remote repair apply can build on those without adding provider mutations.

Completed and published as DevNexus source commit afe5549. Added policy-gated local remote repair apply through the core hosting service, CLI, and MCP. Apply recomputes the plan, only executes allowed local remote add/update actions, preserves undeclared remotes, skips blocked/manual local remote actions clearly, and returns refreshed final status/plan evidence. Verification passed: npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts; npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts src/cli.test.ts src/nexusMcpServer.test.ts src/nexusSharedCheckoutGuard.test.ts; npm run build; git diff --check; npm run check (57 files, 464 tests).

local-123.body:
Completed and published as DevNexus source commit 3c92655. Added generic hosting apply support for provider-gated GitHub repository creation through mocked provider adapters, CLI, and MCP. Apply recomputes the plan, requires an explicit host-local mutation auth profile, rejects missing or wrong actors before provider mutation, reports provider blocked/failed results and returned repository mismatches, returns provider URL facts, and is idempotent when the repository already exists.

Verification passed: focused hosting/CLI/MCP/guard suite (4 files, 95 tests) and npm run build after rebasing over c19b493. Earlier full npm run check passed before the concurrent Mac setup-assistant commit (57 files, 470 tests); after rebasing over c19b493, full check is blocked by the Mac-owned nexusSetupAssistant path tests, not by this hosting slice.

local-124.body: Promoted to ready after dev-nexus:local-123 landed as source commit 3c92655. This slice can now build collaborator drift/repair and pending-invitation behavior on the generic hosting apply entry point and mocked provider mutation contract.

Completed and published as DevNexus source commit 6e42b7f. Added provider-gated access repair apply for declared hosting principals. The generic apply path now includes access_repair actions, requires the provider mutation auth profile, rejects missing or mismatched actors before provider mutation, calls a provider repairAccess adapter with declared principal and required permission, reports provider blocked/failed results, preserves unsupported-principal blockers, and remains idempotent when final status is already satisfied.

Verification passed: npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts; npm run build; npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts src/cli.test.ts src/nexusMcpServer.test.ts src/nexusSharedCheckoutGuard.test.ts (4 files, 98 tests); git diff --check. Full npm run check builds successfully but remains red in the two Mac-owned nexusSetupAssistant path tests on Windows; this lane did not touch those files.

local-125.body: Promoted to ready after dev-nexus:local-124 landed as source commit 6e42b7f. Invitation acceptance can now build on pending-invitation status/plan actions, declared invitee auth profiles, and the generic provider mutation apply pattern.

Completed and published as DevNexus source commit 09513af. Added provider-gated invitation acceptance apply through declared invitee auth profiles. Status now carries provider invitation ids when available; plan actions include the invitation id; apply includes invitation_acceptance by default, validates the invitee auth profile and observed actor before mutation, can discover a missing invitation id through provider listInvitations, calls acceptInvitation, reports missing/expired/provider-blocked invitations distinctly, and is idempotent once final status is satisfied.

Verification passed: npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts; npm run build; git diff --check; npm test -- src/nexusProjectHosting.test.ts src/cli.test.ts src/nexusMcpServer.test.ts src/nexusSharedCheckoutGuard.test.ts (4 files, 101 tests). Full npm run check builds successfully but remains red in the two Mac-owned nexusSetupAssistant path tests on Windows; this lane did not touch those files.

local-128.body:
Product Requirements Document (PRD) created at `docs/tracker-discovery-inbound-sync-prd.md`.

Key decision: preserve local-default eligible-work behavior by default, but add explicit discovery sources and policy-gated inbound import so provider-native GitHub Issues can become canonical local work before cross-host workers select them. Ready for `$to-issues` slicing.

Sliced the cross-tracker discovery and inbound issue sync PRD into `dev-nexus:local-129` through `dev-nexus:local-137`.

Order:
- `local-129` tracker discovery roles and policy defaults
- `local-130` read-only tracker discovery status
- `local-131` eligible-work aggregation from configured discovery sources
- `local-132` linked work-item deduplication across trackers
- `local-133` inbound GitHub-to-local import planning
- `local-134` policy-gated inbound import execution to the local tracker
- `local-135` external issue visibility summaries for agent and target surfaces
- `local-136` fake GitHub inbox discovery/import smoke coverage
- `local-137` blocked HITL decision for dogfood external selection/provider-write/scheduler policy

Important correction from review: live validation must use a mocked or disposable fake project/provider fixture, not `dev-nexus-plexus`, this dogfood project as a live provider target, or any other active project.

Closing as completed during Windows cleanup/reconciliation. Evidence: docs/tracker-discovery-inbound-sync-prd.md exists, target state records the PRD and order, and the PRD has already been sliced into dev-nexus:local-129 through local-137 with the live-validation correction that fake/disposable provider fixtures must be used instead of active projects. Next implementation is local-129, now started in its own worktree.

Full npm run check still fails only in the known two Windows setup-assistant path assertions.

local-138.body:
Implemented on `codex/dev-nexus/mac-setup-assistant-friction` and published to DevNexus `bot/main` as `c19b493`.

Summary:
- Setup plans now render fresh-project guidance with platform placeholder roots such as `$HOME/dev-nexus/<project-id>` or `$env:USERPROFILE\dev-nexus\<project-id>` instead of leaking the current temporary checkout path.
- Setup checks resolve component source roots against the real current-host filesystem, so fixtures and real local checkouts are inspected correctly.
- This fixes the Mac path rendering and source-root fallback failures in `src/nexusSetupAssistant.test.ts`.

Verification:
- `npm test -- src/nexusSetupAssistant.test.ts` passed: 22 tests.
- `npm run check` passed: 57 files, 463 passed, 1 skipped.

local-139.body:
Resolved by the same DevNexus commit as `local-138`: `c19b493` on `bot/main`.

The advisory setup-check failures were caused by component source-root checks being evaluated against platform placeholder paths instead of the actual current-host fixture checkout. With setup checks using the real host filesystem path, the agent MCP target, plugin projection, and agent-session tests now remain warning-classified when their advisory conditions are the only gaps.

Verification:
- `npm test -- src/nexusSetupAssistant.test.ts` passed: 22 tests.
- `npm run check` passed: 57 files, 463 passed, 1 skipped.

local-140.body:
Resolved by DevNexus commit `c19b493` on `bot/main`.

The setup assistant test helper now initializes temporary Git repositories with a test-local `init.defaultBranch=main` config. This avoids repeated Git 2.54 default-branch warning blocks without changing the developer's global Git config or suppressing unrelated command stderr.

Verification:
- `npm test -- src/nexusSetupAssistant.test.ts` passed without the default-branch warning blocks.
- `npm run check` passed: 57 files, 463 passed, 1 skipped.

local-144.body:
Implemented a coordination integration planner fix on branch `codex/local-144-component-integration-repo`, commit `15f07effd348f2785364bffca201c0126ae37873`.

Change summary:
- `getNexusCoordinationIntegrationPlan` now prefers the selected component source repository when resolving the git repository used for target refs and merge analysis.
- Status/handoff git capture keeps its current-path-first behavior.
- Added a regression where the DevNexus project metadata repo and component source repo have different `main` commits; integration planning is invoked from the project repo but must report the component commit and merge the component branch.

Verification:
- Red regression reproduced the project-repo commit before the fix.
- `npm test -- src/nexusCoordination.test.ts` passed (12 tests).
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `npm run build` passed.
- Patched CLI verified the PLexus repro: `coordination integrate ... --component plexus --work-item local-30 --target-branch main --json` returned target commit `764edb5a45ed1fe21cc709f3228b1663025a6b05`, matching `/Users/gabriel.darbord/dev-nexus/sources/plexus`.

Blocker before publishing: full `npm run check` still fails in unrelated `src/nexusSetupAssistant.test.ts` assertions. I filed that as `dev-nexus:local-145`. This branch has not been pushed or integrated.
